Haley L. Hansen of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (415) 512-4381, appearing through CourtCall for defendant and moving party J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiffs and opposing parties SUSAN HILL, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of RAYMOND HILL, Deceased; and RAYMOND WAYNE HILL, MISTY HAGGARD-CARDER; RAESHELLE DAVIS, STEPHEN HILL and REBEKAH RUGNAO, as Wrongful Death Heirs of RAYMOND HILL, Deceased.

RULING: DEFENDANT J-M MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST ALL CLAIMS MADE BY STEPHEN HILL AND RAESHELLE DAVIS
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication Against All Claims Made by Stephen Hill and Raeshelle Davis is DENIED. Defendant failed to present evidence that would require a reasonable trier of fact not to find any underlying material fact more likely than not. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 845.) Plaintiffs' testimony that they have not had recent contact with the Decedent and did not rely on Decedent financially is insufficient to shift the burden as to wrongful death damages. (Depositions of Raeshelle Davis and Stephen Hill, Exs. B-C.) Defendant failed to depose Plaintiffs or conduct written discovery regarding the extent of Plaintiffs' damages. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal. App. 4th 64, 81 [courts can "infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) As for punitive damages, the motion for summary adjudication is denied as the only punitive damages claims are in the survival action by Susan Hill. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The Court did not consider the opposition filed by J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc. or conditional opposition filed by Auto Parts Wholesale for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7B.) The Court denies J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s request for relief based on excusable neglect. The trial date is June 15, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is June 24, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 11, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 29, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 12, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 25, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
